ai_model_router.proof_boundary: Router decisions bound local simulated review and endpoint contracts; they do not prove live model provider availability.

route_decisions.next_valid_move_if_denied: Use metadata-only/manual review or change organization and document-level AI permission.
Use metadata-only/manual review or change organization and document-level AI permission.
Use metadata-only/manual review or change organization and document-level AI permission.

boundary: Trade Readiness Copilot organizes import/export evidence and blockers. It does not provide legal, customs, tariff, tax, CFIA, supplier, buyer, compliance, shipment, or launch advice.

claim_rules.customer_copy: Official/reference sources need a dated refresh record before they support even internal reference use.
Tariff/HS classification requires qualified review before external use.
CFIA/food-import claims stay blocked until a scoped qualified finding exists.
Export-to-Canada readiness needs exporter-side documents, Canadian importer responsibility, and broker/expert review.
Importer-of-record and DDP/non-resident importer responsibility must be confirmed before shipment decisions.
Product, commercial, certificate, proof-of-origin, and shipping documents remain incomplete until reviewed.
Trade agreement, MoU, rules-of-origin, or preference claims need official evidence and qualified review.
Source or commercial rights need a contract, terms, or review record.
Buyer validation needs dated buyer/operator evidence.
Launch readiness remains closed until all product, security, privacy, and human gates are complete.

claims.blocked_display_text: Official/reference sources need a dated refresh record before they support even internal reference use.
Tariff/HS classification requires qualified review before external use.
CFIA/food-import claims stay blocked until a scoped qualified finding exists.
Export-to-Canada readiness needs exporter-side documents, Canadian importer responsibility, and broker/expert review.
Importer-of-record and DDP/non-resident importer responsibility must be confirmed before shipment decisions.
Product, commercial, certificate, proof-of-origin, and shipping documents remain incomplete until reviewed.
Trade agreement, MoU, rules-of-origin, or preference claims need official evidence and qualified review.
Source or commercial rights need a contract, terms, or review record.
Buyer validation needs dated buyer/operator evidence.
Launch readiness remains closed until all product, security, privacy, and human gates are complete.

customer_promise: Before you import or export, know what is missing. Turn a messy trade idea into an evidence-backed readiness packet that shows what is known, what is missing, which claims are blocked, and what review is required before moving forward.

deployment.backups: SQLite and system_review_graph artifacts can be backed up from the mounted /app/system_review_graph volume
external_gates: Provision real managed database/object storage before production
Configure real secret manager and TLS termination before public hosting
Run qualified security/privacy review before external customers
Test backup restore in staging before production
deployment.logs: structured application/audit events are written to generated JSON and SQLite artifacts

model_endpoints.default_behavior: local deterministic simulator; no provider call by default

deployment.monitoring: health endpoints expose app, store, route, and generated-artifact readiness
deployment.proof_boundary: Deployment artifacts make the product hostable; they do not prove a live production environment exists.

manual_no_ai_workflow.next_valid_move: Use operator review and scoped expert-review packet when AI is disabled.
manual_no_ai_workflow.report_disclosure: No AI processing is required. Reports disclose whether AI was used.

private_beta_checklist.item: AI simulated review cannot open gates
AI data policy and model router exist

public_privacy_controls.retention_notice: delete or expire after public quick check
public_privacy_controls.review_boundary: PIPEDA/OWASP/NIST references are control prompts, not legal or security certification claims.

customer_flow: choose beginner start or document mode
triage native text or OCR need
levels: Level 0 beginner starter - no documents, missing-evidence checklist, source/research routing
Level 1 public free tool - no login, limited PDF upload, draft PDF, auto-delete/delete controls
Level 2 free account - saved history and workspace
Level 3 Pro - more packets and review workflows
Level 5 Enterprise/private AI - customer-managed or private hosted model routing
Level 6 opportunity scanner - signal-only research prompts
Level 7 country coverage - support tiers and claim gates
Level 8 transport readiness - freight-forwarder question packets
Level 9 billing/credits - local metering and heavy-job gates
Level 10 agent/API layer - scoped tool manifest
Level 11 traffic-first growth pages - checklists and sample reports
Level 12 research execution - model, web, official, dataset, and expert lanes
Level 13 expert network - scoped review routing
Level 14 business/team workspace - approval board and roles
Level 15 launch operations - private beta controls and outcome loop

redaction_pipeline.proof_boundary: Redaction preview names fields/categories to remove before AI; production redaction requires security/privacy review.

requirements_traceability.boundary: Local session auth and RBAC are implemented for private beta dry-runs; production identity review remains external.
requirements_traceability.name: Identity, organization, permissions

requirements_traceability.boundary: Packet intake creates review state, not import approval.
requirements_traceability.name: Packet intake

requirements_traceability.boundary: No evidence, no claim; stale/reference-only evidence blocks external claims.
requirements_traceability.name: Evidence ledger

requirements_traceability.boundary: Refresh records are evidence inputs; they do not replace qualified review.
requirements_traceability.name: Official source refresh

requirements_traceability.boundary: External-world claims stay blocked until evidence and human gates close.
requirements_traceability.name: Claim and blocker engine

requirements_traceability.boundary: Router controls local simulated review and endpoint contracts; no live provider call is made by default.
requirements_traceability.name: AI processing and model routing

requirements_traceability.boundary: AI reviewers create findings and next moves only; they cannot open gates.
requirements_traceability.name: AI simulated reviewers

requirements_traceability.boundary: Operator UX is local/private-beta oriented.
requirements_traceability.name: Operator UX

requirements_traceability.boundary: Expert review packet is scoped; no advice or approval is implied by the app.
requirements_traceability.name: Expert review UX

requirements_traceability.boundary: Reports disclose blockers and AI involvement; they are not certificates.
requirements_traceability.name: Reports and exports

requirements_traceability.boundary: Customer UX is private-beta candidate, not public launch proof.
requirements_traceability.name: Customer UX

requirements_traceability.boundary: Admin controls are local/private-beta implementation surfaces.
requirements_traceability.name: Admin UX

requirements_traceability.boundary: Policies and deletion workflow exist; qualified privacy/legal review remains required before hosted customer use.
requirements_traceability.name: Privacy and data controls

requirements_traceability.boundary: Production identity, TLS, secret manager, malware scanning, and security review remain external gates.
requirements_traceability.name: Security

requirements_traceability.boundary: Local JSON/SQLite audit exists; production observability stack remains a deployment gate.
requirements_traceability.name: Audit and observability

requirements_traceability.boundary: Hostable artifacts exist; no live production environment is claimed.
requirements_traceability.name: Deployment environments

requirements_traceability.boundary: Acceptance proves local product behavior and generated artifacts, not external legal/compliance approval.
requirements_traceability.name: Testing and acceptance gates

requirements_traceability.boundary: Public quick check is no-login draft PDF generation with delete controls; it is not approval, advice, shipment readiness, or persistent account history.
requirements_traceability.name: Public trade readiness quick check

requirements_traceability.boundary: Exporter packet creates evidence gaps and review packets only.
requirements_traceability.name: Create Export-to-Canada packet

requirements_traceability.boundary: Missing buyer/importer creates a blocker; presence is not buyer validation.
requirements_traceability.name: Identify Canadian buyer/importer

requirements_traceability.boundary: Incoterms classify responsibility path only; they do not prove legal or customs readiness.
requirements_traceability.name: Capture Incoterms or delivery responsibility

requirements_traceability.boundary: Exporter/DDP/non-resident importer responsibility triggers broker review gates.
requirements_traceability.name: Mark Canadian import responsibility

requirements_traceability.boundary: Canadian official/reference checks remain evidence only until qualified review.
requirements_traceability.name: Check Canadian-side readiness blockers

requirements_traceability.boundary: Exporter-side readiness is a document-gap lane, not country legal advice.
requirements_traceability.name: Check exporter-side document readiness

requirements_traceability.boundary: Buyer packet is a draft question/evidence packet and cannot claim buyer validation.
requirements_traceability.name: Generate buyer-ready packet

requirements_traceability.boundary: Broker packet asks for scoped review; it is not broker advice or clearance.
requirements_traceability.name: Generate Canadian broker-review packet

requirements_traceability.boundary: All external-world claims remain blocked without qualified evidence and human gates.
requirements_traceability.name: Block compliance, tariff, CFIA, buyer, and shipment claims

requirements_traceability.boundary: Starter mode creates a missing-evidence packet and checklist; it is not advice or approval.
requirements_traceability.name: Beginner no-documents starter flow

requirements_traceability.boundary: PDF triage detects local text/OCR needs and asks for confirmation; it does not verify document authenticity.
requirements_traceability.name: PDF triage and native text extraction decision

requirements_traceability.boundary: User confirmation records draft context only; human experts still own external claims.
requirements_traceability.name: User confirmation before extracted fields become packet context

requirements_traceability.boundary: The contract maps sources, snapshots, diffs, and packet impacts; live source truth and qualified review remain external gates.
requirements_traceability.name: Intelligence Hub policy/source monitoring contract

requirements_traceability.boundary: Opportunity rows are signals and research prompts, not recommendations, demand proof, margin proof, or buyer validation.
requirements_traceability.name: Opportunity scanner with research gates

requirements_traceability.boundary: Coverage tiers describe product support level only; they are not country-specific legal, customs, tariff, or compliance correctness.
requirements_traceability.name: Country coverage tiers

requirements_traceability.boundary: Transport readiness prepares freight-forwarder questions; it does not guarantee routes, costs, carrier choice, or shipment readiness.
requirements_traceability.name: Transport readiness lane

requirements_traceability.boundary: Billing controls define plan, credit, and heavy-job authorization only; live checkout and invoicing are disabled.
requirements_traceability.name: Billing and credit controls

requirements_traceability.boundary: Agents can create packets and reports through product gates only; no live public API gateway or claim-opening tool is exposed.
requirements_traceability.name: Agent/API contract

requirements_traceability.boundary: Traffic/checklist pages educate and route to the tool; they are not legal, customs, tariff, supplier, buyer, or shipment advice.
requirements_traceability.name: Traffic-first public pages

requirements_traceability.boundary: All locally implementable stages have routes, APIs, and artifacts; real-world approvals remain external gates.
requirements_traceability.name: All local product stages

requirements_traceability.boundary: Research routing chooses model-prior, web, official-source, dataset, and expert/user lanes; it does not prove the external facts.
requirements_traceability.name: Research and data execution routing

requirements_traceability.boundary: The product can prepare and route scoped expert packets; real qualified findings remain external evidence.
requirements_traceability.name: Expert network and review routing

requirements_traceability.boundary: Team workspace roles and approval boards are local coordination surfaces, not real owner approval.
requirements_traceability.name: Business/team workspace

requirements_traceability.boundary: Gateway tools execute local product operations only; no public API, credentials, payments, or claim-opening authority are exposed.
requirements_traceability.name: Agent API gateway executor

requirements_traceability.boundary: Launch operations are ready for private-beta review; production deployment and public launch require owner approval.
requirements_traceability.name: Launch operations and private beta controls

requirements_traceability.boundary: Local operations mutate product state, reports, and ledgers; they still do not create external legal, customs, tariff, buyer, supplier, payment, or launch approvals.
requirements_traceability.name: Executable local product operations

questions: Does the current evidence support any scoped statement about Canada import/food requirements?
Which claims remain blocked and why?
What evidence or source refresh is required next?
review_requests.scope: Review evidence and claim language for Canada import, food, and tariff readiness.

review_templates.name: Canada food import/source readiness
questions: Does the current evidence support any scoped statement about Canada import/food requirements?
Which claims remain blocked and why?
What evidence or source refresh is required next?
review_templates.scope: Review evidence and claim language for Canada import, food, and tariff readiness.

review_templates.name: Privacy and private-beta security review
questions: Are org isolation and scoped expert links operating as expected?
Are data deletion and audit records sufficient for private beta?
What controls remain before hosted external use?
review_templates.scope: Review private-beta controls, privacy notice, deletion workflow, upload controls, audit, and access boundaries.

security_controls.ai_data_policy: organization policy controls allowed modes, sensitivity, endpoint routing, retention, and no-AI fallback
security_controls.audit: packet, AI, review, export, deletion, and permission events are recorded
security_controls.authentication: local session auth implemented for private beta dry-run
security_controls.csrf: local form token available for browser unsafe actions
security_controls.model_router: per-evidence model routing denies disallowed modes and records endpoint decisions
security_controls.no_ai_mode: manual workflow remains available when AI is disabled at org or evidence level
security_controls.organization_isolation: packet queries filter by organization unless operator/admin
security_controls.rbac: role permission matrix enforced by route handlers
security_controls.redaction_pipeline: sensitive evidence is marked for redaction before AI processing
security_controls.route_traversal: route-specific artifact base directories with resolved-path checks
security_controls.scoped_expert_links: review tokens grant packet-scoped access only

upload_validation.malicious_html_policy: reject script-bearing metadata and always HTML-escape display
upload_validation.ocr_cost_gate: OCR pages are estimated and require explicit user approval before any external OCR/AI charge
upload_validation.parser_sandbox: local bounded parser only; no shell execution, no remote fetch, no direct file serving
upload_validation.pdf_triage: native text extraction attempted, OCR need flagged, user confirmation required
upload_validation.rate_limit: local policy bucket: public_quick_check allows 6 uploads per 10 minutes per browser/IP before hosted deployment
